trans-unit.source: Sketch Map Tool Exercise 1 - Workflow Exercise
trans-unit.target: Sketch Map Tool Exercise 1 - Workflow Exercise

trans-unit.source: Explore the whole workflow of mapping with the Sketch Map Tool, from first ideas about what you like to map to the possible digital results.
trans-unit.target: Explore the whole workflow of mapping with the Sketch Map Tool, from first ideas about what you like to map to the possible digital results.

trans-unit.source: Characteristics of the exercise
trans-unit.target: Characteristics of the exercise

trans-unit.source: :::{card} __Aim of this exercise:__ ^^^ Get familiar with the Sketch Map Tool and its whole workflow from map creation to the results. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{card} __Aim of this exercise:__ ^^^ Get familiar with the Sketch Map Tool and its whole workflow from map creation to the results. :::

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Type of trainings exercise:__ ^^^ This exercise can be used in online and presence training and is focused on an hands-on experience with the Sketch Map Tool. :::
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Type of trainings exercise:__ ^^^ This exercise can be used in online and presence training and is focused on an hands-on experience with the Sketch Map Tool. :::

trans-unit.source: planners/facilitators, as well as practitioners involved in the preparation of the field campaign or collecting the data
trans-unit.target: planners/facilitators, as well as practitioners involved in the preparation of the field campaign or collecting the data

trans-unit.source: GIS Beginners-level: no specific knowledge about QGIS/uMAP required
trans-unit.target: GIS Beginners-level: no specific knowledge about QGIS/uMAP required

trans-unit.source: __Phase of participatory / community mapping__
trans-unit.target: __Phase of participatory / community mapping__

trans-unit.source: preparing participatory mapping / facilitating participatory mapping/ analysing participatory mapping
trans-unit.target: preparing participatory mapping / facilitating participatory mapping/ analysing participatory mapping

trans-unit.source: Between 2 and 2,5 hours (depending on number of groups).
trans-unit.target: Between 2 and 2,5 hours (depending on number of groups).

trans-unit.source: Available data
trans-unit.target: Available data

trans-unit.source: [Introductory slides on the Sketch Map Tool](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool/PPP/Introduction_Sketch_Map_Tool_21_03.pptx)
trans-unit.target: [Introductory slides on the Sketch Map Tool](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool/PPP/Introduction_Sketch_Map_Tool_21_03.pptx)

trans-unit.source: Case Studies Maps: [Case Study Map 1](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Sketch_Map_Tool_Exercise_2-3/Case_1_Sudan.zip), [Case Study Map 2](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Sketch_Map_Tool_Exercise_2-3/Case_2_Bolivien.zip), [Case Study Map 3](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Sketch_Map_Tool_Exercise_2-3/Case_3_Port_Harcourt.zip)
trans-unit.target: Case Studies Maps: [Case Study Map 1](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Sketch_Map_Tool_Exercise_2-3/Case_1_Sudan.zip), [Case Study Map 2](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Sketch_Map_Tool_Exercise_2-3/Case_2_Bolivien.zip), [Case Study Map 3](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Sketch_Map_Tool_Exercise_2-3/Case_3_Port_Harcourt.zip)

trans-unit.source: [Case Study 1 marked Maps and Sketch Map Tool Outputs](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Sketch_Map_Tool_Exercise_2-3/Case_1_marked_maps_results.zip)
trans-unit.target: [Case Study 1 marked Maps and Sketch Map Tool Outputs](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Sketch_Map_Tool_Exercise_2-3/Case_1_marked_maps_results.zip)

trans-unit.source: [Printable exercise factsheet](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Factsheet_printing_%20Ex_1.pdf)
trans-unit.target: [Printable exercise factsheet](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Factsheet_printing_%20Ex_1.pdf)

trans-unit.source: Instructions for the trainers
trans-unit.target: Instructions for the trainers

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__

trans-unit.source: Prepare the training
trans-unit.target: Prepare the training

trans-unit.source: Online access and devices (PC) to be able to use the Sketch Map Tool to create maps online, upload and download them.
trans-unit.target: Online access and devices (PC) to be able to use the Sketch Map Tool to create maps online, upload and download them.

trans-unit.source: Possibility to print the maps, smart phones to take the photos, and the possibility to upload the pictures to the Sketch Map Tool.
trans-unit.target: Possibility to print the maps, smart phones to take the photos, and the possibility to upload the pictures to the Sketch Map Tool.

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the provided material for the exercise and the Sketch Map Tool in general.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the provided material for the exercise and the Sketch Map Tool in general.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a board (it can be a white board/flipchart or digital e.g. Miro-board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a board (it can be a white board/flipchart or digital e.g. Miro-board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md) for some general tips on training conduction
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md) for some general tips on training conduction

trans-unit.source: Conduct the training:
trans-unit.target: Conduct the training:

trans-unit.source: __Introduction:__
trans-unit.target: __Introduction:__

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea, the aim, and the general workflow of the Sketch Map Tool before starting the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea, the aim, and the general workflow of the Sketch Map Tool before starting the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide access to the needed material and form groups (at least 3 people each to discuss together; ideal group size is 3 to 6 participants).
trans-unit.target: Provide access to the needed material and form groups (at least 3 people each to discuss together; ideal group size is 3 to 6 participants).

trans-unit.source: __Time for group work:__
trans-unit.target: __Time for group work:__

trans-unit.source: Check-in with the groups and ask if there are questions or problems.
trans-unit.target: Check-in with the groups and ask if there are questions or problems.

trans-unit.source: Prepare the presentation tool for the Wrap-Up.
trans-unit.target: Prepare the presentation tool for the Wrap-Up.

trans-unit.source: __Wrap up:__
trans-unit.target: __Wrap up:__

trans-unit.source: All groups present their findings (each 5 min, make it short but show the maps they are talking about).
trans-unit.target: All groups present their findings (each 5 min, make it short but show the maps they are talking about).

trans-unit.source: Discuss the benefits, as well as challenges and problems in the use of the Sketch Map Tool
trans-unit.target: Discuss the benefits, as well as challenges and problems in the use of the Sketch Map Tool

trans-unit.source: Leave some time to for Open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time to for Open questions.

trans-unit.source: What you need to know - background information
trans-unit.target: What you need to know - background information

trans-unit.source: Basic functionalities of the SMT
trans-unit.target: Basic functionalities of the SMT

trans-unit.source: The [Sketch Map Tool](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org) is a easy-to-use tool for participatory mapping. It involves the offline collection of data, and the digitisation and georeferencing of the collected local data. The low-tech solution is designed to simplify the collection and analysis of local spatial knowledge and perceptions through the use of pens and paper maps: the so-called "Sketch Maps". The Sketch Map Tool is an open-source web application. It facilitates the creation, usage and the digitisation and analysis of paper-based maps with OpenStreetMap or satellite data.
trans-unit.target: The [Sketch Map Tool](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org) is a easy-to-use tool for participatory mapping. It involves the offline collection of data, and the digitisation and georeferencing of the collected local data. The low-tech solution is designed to simplify the collection and analysis of local spatial knowledge and perceptions through the use of pens and paper maps: the so-called "Sketch Maps". The Sketch Map Tool is an open-source web application. It facilitates the creation, usage and the digitisation and analysis of paper-based maps with OpenStreetMap or satellite data.

trans-unit.source: The first step is the creation of the paper-based maps. Participants can choose between basemaps containing [OpenStreetMap (OSM)](www.openstreetmap.org) data or basemaps containing satellite images. In addition, the tool can evaluate how well-suited an area of the OpenStreetMap (OSM) basemap is for participatory mapping. This evaluation is based on a quality analysis of the OpenStreetMap (OSM) data using the [HeiGIT ohsome quality analyst](https://heigit.org/de/big-spatial-data-analytics/ohsome-3/ohsome-quality-analyst-oqt/).
trans-unit.target: The first step is the creation of the paper-based maps. Participants can choose between basemaps containing [OpenStreetMap (OSM)](www.openstreetmap.org) data or basemaps containing satellite images. In addition, the tool can evaluate how well-suited an area of the OpenStreetMap (OSM) basemap is for participatory mapping. This evaluation is based on a quality analysis of the OpenStreetMap (OSM) data using the [HeiGIT ohsome quality analyst](https://heigit.org/de/big-spatial-data-analytics/ohsome-3/ohsome-quality-analyst-oqt/).

trans-unit.source: Usually, the data is collected on site and offline through participatory mapping. For this, you only need participants, printed Sketch Maps and pens to draw markings on the Sketch Maps.
trans-unit.target: Usually, the data is collected on site and offline through participatory mapping. For this, you only need participants, printed Sketch Maps and pens to draw markings on the Sketch Maps.

trans-unit.source: After mapping, the Sketch Map Tool supports the digitisation of the Sketch Maps. Upon uploading the pictures or scans of the marked Sketch Maps, the Sketch Map Tool initiates an automatic georeferencing and colour-detection process. The tool uses a combination of computer vision algorithms and novel AI models to extract Sketch Maps from photos and detect markings on them. The results can be downloaded in various geodata formats. The results can then be used for further analysis in Geographic Information System software (such as QGIS).
trans-unit.target: After mapping, the Sketch Map Tool supports the digitisation of the Sketch Maps. Upon uploading the pictures or scans of the marked Sketch Maps, the Sketch Map Tool initiates an automatic georeferencing and colour-detection process. The tool uses a combination of computer vision algorithms and novel AI models to extract Sketch Maps from photos and detect markings on them. The results can be downloaded in various geodata formats. The results can then be used for further analysis in Geographic Information System software (such as QGIS).

trans-unit.source: What does 'georeferenced' mean?
trans-unit.target: What does 'georeferenced' mean?

trans-unit.source: A non-georeferenced image
trans-unit.target: A non-georeferenced image

trans-unit.source: A georeferenced image – GeoTIFF
trans-unit.target: A georeferenced image – GeoTIFF

trans-unit.source: Just an image, even if you load it in QGis and give it a coordination system it can´t be located and the image is shown somewhere in the Atlantic.
trans-unit.target: Just an image, even if you load it in QGis and give it a coordination system it can´t be located and the image is shown somewhere in the Atlantic.

trans-unit.source: The file contains information about its coordinate system and its location, so you can combine it other geodata e.g. with GPS data.
trans-unit.target: The file contains information about its coordinate system and its location, so you can combine it other geodata e.g. with GPS data.

trans-unit.source: Background Information on UMAP and QGIS in comparison
trans-unit.target: Background Information on UMAP and QGIS in comparison

trans-unit.source: UMAP is an online platform that allows users to create custom maps with OpenStreetMap (OSM) as a basemap layer. No installation or registration is necessary to use the platform. This enables users to quickly gain an intuitive overview of their data. Users can customize the appearance of the map and share it with others. It's particularly useful for collaborative mapping projects, quick visualization of geographic data, and creating custom maps tailored to specific needs.
trans-unit.target: UMAP is an online platform that allows users to create custom maps with OpenStreetMap (OSM) as a basemap layer. No installation or registration is necessary to use the platform. This enables users to quickly gain an intuitive overview of their data. Users can customize the appearance of the map and share it with others. It's particularly useful for collaborative mapping projects, quick visualization of geographic data, and creating custom maps tailored to specific needs.

trans-unit.source: Feature
trans-unit.target: Feature

trans-unit.source: QGIS
trans-unit.target: QGIS

trans-unit.source: uMap
trans-unit.target: uMap

trans-unit.source: Type
trans-unit.target: Type

trans-unit.source: Desktop Geographic Information System (GIS) software
trans-unit.target: Desktop Geographic Information System (GIS) software

trans-unit.source: Online mapping platform
trans-unit.target: Online mapping platform

trans-unit.source: Accessibility
trans-unit.target: Accessibility

trans-unit.source: Requires installation on a desktop computer
trans-unit.target: Requires installation on a desktop computer

trans-unit.source: Accessible online via web browser
trans-unit.target: Accessible online via web browser

trans-unit.source: Purpose
trans-unit.target: Purpose

trans-unit.source: Comprehensive GIS software for spatial data analysis
trans-unit.target: Comprehensive GIS software for spatial data analysis

trans-unit.source: Interactive mapping tool for creating custom maps
trans-unit.target: Interactive mapping tool for creating custom maps

trans-unit.source: Data Import
trans-unit.target: Data Import

trans-unit.source: Supports various data formats (shapefiles, geodatabases, raster data, etc.)
trans-unit.target: Supports various data formats (shapefiles, geodatabases, raster data, etc.)

trans-unit.source: Vector data only, user input via map canvas, some geodata formats (geojson, .gpx, .kml, .osm)
trans-unit.target: Vector data only, user input via map canvas, some geodata formats (geojson, .gpx, .kml, .osm)

trans-unit.source: Data Visualisation and Analysis
trans-unit.target: Data Visualisation and Analysis

trans-unit.source: Offers extensive visualisation and analysis capabilities; Provides a wide range of geospatial analysis tools
trans-unit.target: Offers extensive visualisation and analysis capabilities; Provides a wide range of geospatial analysis tools

trans-unit.source: Focuses on the creation of interactive maps with custom elements; No analysis tools
trans-unit.target: Focuses on the creation of interactive maps with custom elements; No analysis tools

trans-unit.source: Output
trans-unit.target: Output

trans-unit.source: Printable map with all necessary map elements
trans-unit.target: Printable map with all necessary map elements

trans-unit.source: URL to share map online
trans-unit.target: URL to share map online

trans-unit.source: Step-by step introduction for participants
trans-unit.target: Step-by step introduction for participants

trans-unit.source: If you experience any problems during your use of the [Sketch Map Tool](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org/) please take a look at the [help page](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org/help).
trans-unit.target: If you experience any problems during your use of the [Sketch Map Tool](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org/) please take a look at the [help page](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org/help).

trans-unit.source: 1. Start discussion
trans-unit.target: 1. Start discussion

trans-unit.source: Prepare and discuss in your group.
trans-unit.target: Prepare and discuss in your group.

trans-unit.source: Take a look at information about your case-study. The images included might give you an idea of how the community might look like.
trans-unit.target: Take a look at information about your case-study. The images included might give you an idea of how the community might look like.

trans-unit.source: What would do you want to map? Which data do you want to collect? You can decide if you want to map hazards (e.g. the flood extent), vulnerabilities (e.g. poor infrastructure) or capacities.
trans-unit.target: What would do you want to map? Which data do you want to collect? You can decide if you want to map hazards (e.g. the flood extent), vulnerabilities (e.g. poor infrastructure) or capacities.

trans-unit.source: Scroll through the help-page of the Sketch Map Tool
trans-unit.target: Scroll through the help-page of the Sketch Map Tool

trans-unit.source: 2. Create a map in the Sketch Map Tool
trans-unit.target: 2. Create a map in the Sketch Map Tool

trans-unit.source: Open the [Sketch Map Tool](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org/)
trans-unit.target: Open the [Sketch Map Tool](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org/)

trans-unit.source: Zoom in and select your map area. Try different zoom levels and change the orientation.
trans-unit.target: Zoom in and select your map area. Try different zoom levels and change the orientation.

trans-unit.source: Create a map in an A4-size (in future cases, you can choose other Sketch Map sizes).
trans-unit.target: Create a map in an A4-size (in future cases, you can choose other Sketch Map sizes).

trans-unit.source: 3. Mapping
trans-unit.target: 3. Mapping

trans-unit.source: Discuss in your group what and how you would like to map. Decide if you focus on larger areas, on streets (or lines), or particular objects, so your mapping is similar and the results can be compared. Please take a look at our [help page](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org/help) for tips on mapping.
trans-unit.target: Discuss in your group what and how you would like to map. Decide if you focus on larger areas, on streets (or lines), or particular objects, so your mapping is similar and the results can be compared. Please take a look at our [help page](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org/help) for tips on mapping.

trans-unit.source: Try to identify realistic places for what ever you want to map. Alternatively, just mark the map with different shapes in different sizes and colours to explore how the digitisation will look like.
trans-unit.target: Try to identify realistic places for what ever you want to map. Alternatively, just mark the map with different shapes in different sizes and colours to explore how the digitisation will look like.

trans-unit.source: 4. Digitise
trans-unit.target: 4. Digitise

trans-unit.source: Take a photo of your map. Please take a look at our [help page](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org/help) for tips on how to take the picture.
trans-unit.target: Take a photo of your map. Please take a look at our [help page](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org/help) for tips on how to take the picture.

trans-unit.source: Upload the photo or scan to the [Sketch Map Tool](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org/).
trans-unit.target: Upload the photo or scan to the [Sketch Map Tool](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org/).

trans-unit.source: Download the results to the Sketch Map Tool.
trans-unit.target: Download the results to the Sketch Map Tool.

trans-unit.source: 5. Open your results in QGIS or uMap
trans-unit.target: 5. Open your results in QGIS or uMap

trans-unit.source: You can choose to use either QGIS (__Option A__) or [uMap](https://umap.openstreetmap.fr/en/) (__Option B__) for the next part of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: You can choose to use either QGIS (__Option A__) or [uMap](https://umap.openstreetmap.fr/en/) (__Option B__) for the next part of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Option A: Open your results in QGIS :open:
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Option A: Open your results in QGIS :open:

trans-unit.source: A: Open your results in QGIS
trans-unit.target: A: Open your results in QGIS

trans-unit.source: __Open QGIS__
trans-unit.target: __Open QGIS__

trans-unit.source: Open QGIS and navigate to `Project` -> `New` and click on `Save`. Navigate to the folder where you want to save your project, give it a name and click `Save` again. When working in QGIS always remember to save your project every now and then.
trans-unit.target: Open QGIS and navigate to `Project` -> `New` and click on `Save`. Navigate to the folder where you want to save your project, give it a name and click `Save` again. When working in QGIS always remember to save your project every now and then.

trans-unit.source: __Add a Basemap__
trans-unit.target: __Add a Basemap__

trans-unit.source: For a better overview and orientation, it is always helpful to add a basemap to your project and put your situation in a spatial context. Navigate to the browser `Browser` Panel > `XYZ Tiles`, and open the dropdown by clicking on it. Next, select OpenStreetMap or another basemap.
trans-unit.target: For a better overview and orientation, it is always helpful to add a basemap to your project and put your situation in a spatial context. Navigate to the browser `Browser` Panel > `XYZ Tiles`, and open the dropdown by clicking on it. Next, select OpenStreetMap or another basemap.

trans-unit.source: Click [here](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_basemaps_wiki.html#standard-qgis-basemaps) for more information on basemaps and how toa dd them to your project.
trans-unit.target: Click [here](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_basemaps_wiki.html#standard-qgis-basemaps) for more information on basemaps and how toa dd them to your project.

trans-unit.source: __Load your results in QGIS__
trans-unit.target: __Load your results in QGIS__

trans-unit.source: First, unzip the folder you downloaded from the Sketch Map Tool website. In it, you will find a vector file (`geojson`) and a raster file (`geotiff`, `tiff`, or `tif`).
trans-unit.target: First, unzip the folder you downloaded from the Sketch Map Tool website. In it, you will find a vector file (`geojson`) and a raster file (`geotiff`, `tiff`, or `tif`).

trans-unit.source: Load both files into QGIS by dragging them onto QGIS. Be aware which files are at the top, because you might not be able to see the layers which are below. If it is your first time using QGIS, take your time to familiarise yourself with the [QGIS interface](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_interface_wiki.html).
trans-unit.target: Load both files into QGIS by dragging them onto QGIS. Be aware which files are at the top, because you might not be able to see the layers which are below. If it is your first time using QGIS, take your time to familiarise yourself with the [QGIS interface](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_interface_wiki.html).

trans-unit.source: Take a look at the detected markings, compare it with your map. Which differences can you see? Are some markings missing in the Sketch Map Tool results?
trans-unit.target: Take a look at the detected markings, compare it with your map. Which differences can you see? Are some markings missing in the Sketch Map Tool results?

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Option B: Open your results in uMAP :open:
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Option B: Open your results in uMAP :open:

trans-unit.source: B: Open your results in uMap
trans-unit.target: B: Open your results in uMap

trans-unit.source: Open the browser of your choice, navigate to the [uMap Website](https://umap.openstreetmap.fr/en/) and click on the large green button: `Create a map`.
trans-unit.target: Open the browser of your choice, navigate to the [uMap Website](https://umap.openstreetmap.fr/en/) and click on the large green button: `Create a map`.

trans-unit.source: Right above your map canvas, you can click in "`Untitled map`" to edit your map properties. Give your map a title and a short description.
trans-unit.target: Right above your map canvas, you can click in "`Untitled map`" to edit your map properties. Give your map a title and a short description.

trans-unit.source: You can load your data into the map frame by clicking on the upwards-arrow button ![](/fig/umap_upload_data_icon.png) in the toolbar on the right side of the website. The `Import data` panel will open on the right hand side. Click on `Select file` and navigate to your vector output from the SKetch Map Tool ("Schuld_Ahr-tal_sketch-map_Ex4.geojson") and click on `Open`. uMap will automatically detect that your file is a geojson/vector file . Click on `Import` to load your data to your map canvas.
trans-unit.target: You can load your data into the map frame by clicking on the upwards-arrow button ![](/fig/umap_upload_data_icon.png) in the toolbar on the right side of the website. The `Import data` panel will open on the right hand side. Click on `Select file` and navigate to your vector output from the SKetch Map Tool ("Schuld_Ahr-tal_sketch-map_Ex4.geojson") and click on `Open`. uMap will automatically detect that your file is a geojson/vector file . Click on `Import` to load your data to your map canvas.

trans-unit.source: Click in the upper right corner on `Save`. If you are not logged in, you will be provided with an URL where you can access your map any time in the future. Save it somewhere or send it to your email.
trans-unit.target: Click in the upper right corner on `Save`. If you are not logged in, you will be provided with an URL where you can access your map any time in the future. Save it somewhere or send it to your email.

trans-unit.source: 6. Wrap-up: What are your experiences with the Sketch Map Tool? - Discuss:
trans-unit.target: 6. Wrap-up: What are your experiences with the Sketch Map Tool? - Discuss:

trans-unit.source: Take notes and prepare a short presentation about your map area, the markings, and how the results look like at the end. Bring them back in the big group to wrap the exercise together.
trans-unit.target: Take notes and prepare a short presentation about your map area, the markings, and how the results look like at the end. Bring them back in the big group to wrap the exercise together.

trans-unit.source: Did you discover any problems or challenges during this exercise?
trans-unit.target: Did you discover any problems or challenges during this exercise?

trans-unit.source: What is the greatest benefit of the Sketch Map Tool from your perspective?
trans-unit.target: What is the greatest benefit of the Sketch Map Tool from your perspective?

trans-unit.source: Are their any open questions?
trans-unit.target: Are their any open questions?
